title: नरसिंह अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली
devanagariText:
॥ श्री नरसिंह अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली ॥

ॐ नरसिंहाय नमः।
ॐ महासिंहाय नमः।
ॐ दिव्यसिंहाय नमः।
ॐ महाबलाय नमः।
ॐ उग्रसिंहाय नमः।
ॐ महादेवाय नमः।
ॐ स्तम्भजाय नमः।
ॐ उग्रलोचनाय नमः।
ॐ रौद्राय नमः।
ॐ सर्वाद्भुताय नमः॥ १०॥

ॐ श्रीमते नमः।
ॐ योगानन्दाय नमः।
ॐ त्रिविक्रमाय नमः।
ॐ हरये नमः।
ॐ कोलाहलाय नमः।
ॐ चक्रिणे नमः।
ॐ विजयाय नमः।
ॐ जयवर्धनाय नमः।
ॐ पञ्चाननाय नमः।
ॐ परब्रह्मणे नमः॥ २०॥

ॐ अघोराय नमः।
ॐ घोरविक्रमाय नमः।
ॐ ज्वालामुखाय नमः।
ॐ ज्वालमालिने नमः।
ॐ महाज्वालाय नमः।
ॐ महाप्रभवे नमः।
ॐ नित्यानन्दाय नमः।
ॐ निरालम्बाय नमः।
ॐ स्वतन्त्राय नमः।
ॐ गर्वध्वंसिने नमः॥ ३०॥

ॐ शरभाय नमः।
ॐ भूताग्रजाय नमः।
ॐ देवाय नमः।
ॐ अग्निनेत्राय नमः।
ॐ रथाङ्गपाणये नमः।
ॐ सुवर्चसे नमः।
ॐ शाश्वताय नमः।
ॐ तुष्टाय नमः।
ॐ वज्रदंष्ट्राय नमः।
ॐ वज्रनखाय नमः॥ ४०॥

ॐ महाकायाय नमः।
ॐ महारौद्राय नमः।
ॐ महाबाहवे नमः।
ॐ महाप्रभाय नमः।
ॐ कालज्ञाय नमः।
ॐ कालनेमिघ्नाय नमः।
ॐ करालवदनाय नमः।
ॐ शूराय नमः।
ॐ विदारणाय नमः।
ॐ अमिताय नमः॥ ५०॥

ॐ नारायणाय नमः।
ॐ दैत्यसूदनाय नमः।
ॐ मृत्युमृत्यवे नमः।
ॐ सद्गतये नमः।
ॐ अनन्ताय नमः।
ॐ वसुदेवाय नमः।
ॐ विष्णवे नमः।
ॐ प्रह्लादपालकाय नमः।
ॐ शङ्खधारिणे नमः।
ॐ गदापाणये नमः॥ ६०॥

ॐ चक्रधारिणे नमः।
ॐ पद्महस्ताय नमः।
ॐ वनमालिने नमः।
ॐ पीताम्बरधराय नमः।
ॐ श्रीवत्साङ्काय नमः।
ॐ कौस्तुभधराय नमः।
ॐ गरुडध्वजाय नमः।
ॐ क्षीराब्धिनिलयाय नमः।
ॐ शेषशायिने नमः।
ॐ पुण्डरीकाक्षाय नमः॥ ७०॥

ॐ लोकनाथाय नमः।
ॐ जगत्पतये नमः।
ॐ सुब्रह्मण्याय नमः।
ॐ भक्तवत्सलाय नमः।
ॐ सर्वज्ञाय नमः।
ॐ सर्वपालकाय नमः।
ॐ ब्रह्मरूपाय नमः।
ॐ शिवरूपाय नमः।
ॐ विश्वरूपाय नमः।
ॐ सर्वाधाराय नमः॥ ८०॥

ॐ जगद्व्यापिने नमः।
ॐ जगत्कर्त्रे नमः।
ॐ जगद्धर्त्रे नमः।
ॐ जगत्संहर्त्रे नमः।
ॐ परेशाय नमः।
ॐ परमात्मने नमः।
ॐ पापविमोचनाय नमः।
ॐ मनोवेगिने नमः।
ॐ अमेयाय नमः।
ॐ दुष्टसंहारकाय नमः॥ ९०॥

ॐ सर्वशस्त्रधराय नमः।
ॐ सर्वविद्याविशारदाय नमः।
ॐ भक्तार्तिनाशनाय नमः।
ॐ कामितार्थप्रदाय नमः।
ॐ सुखदाय नमः।
ॐ सर्वमङ्गलाय नमः।
ॐ हिरण्यकशिपुच्छेत्रे नमः।
ॐ महाहिरण्यनखाय नमः।
ॐ सर्वलोकधराय नमः।
ॐ अव्ययाय नमः॥ १००॥

ॐ महारूपाय नमः।
ॐ महाप्रभवे नमः।
ॐ अनन्तरूपाय नमः।
ॐ कालरूपाय नमः।
ॐ दयानिधये नमः।
ॐ लक्ष्मीनृसिंहाय नमः।
ॐ सर्वसिद्धिप्रदाय नमः।
ॐ श्रीनृसिंहपरब्रह्मणे नमः॥ १०८॥

॥ इति श्री नरसिंह अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Narasimha Ashtottara Shatanamavali ||

Om Narasimhaya Namah.
Om Mahasimhaya Namah.
Om Divyasimhaya Namah.
Om Mahabalaya Namah.
Om Ugrasimhaya Namah.
Om Mahadevaya Namah.
Om Stambhajaya Namah.
Om Ugralochanaya Namah.
Om Raudraya Namah.
Om Sarvadbhutaya Namah. || 10 ||

Om Shrimate Namah.
Om Yoganandaya Namah.
Om Trivikramaya Namah.
Om Haraye Namah.
Om Kolahalaya Namah.
Om Chakrine Namah.
Om Vijayaya Namah.
Om Jayavardhanaya Namah.
Om Panchananya Namah.
Om Parabrahmane Namah. || 20 ||

Om Aghoraya Namah.
Om Ghoravikramaya Namah.
Om Jwalamukhaya Namah.
Om Jwalamaline Namah.
Om Mahajwalaya Namah.
Om Mahaprabhave Namah.
Om Nityanandaya Namah.
Om Niralambaya Namah.
Om Svatantraya Namah.
Om Garvadhvamsine Namah. || 30 ||

Om Sharabhaya Namah.
Om Bhutagrajaya Namah.
Om Devaya Namah.
Om Agni-netraya Namah.
Om Rathanga-panaye Namah.
Om Suvarchase Namah.
Om Shashvataya Namah.
Om Tushtaya Namah.
Om Vajradamshtraya Namah.
Om Vajranakhaya Namah. || 40 ||

Om Mahakayaya Namah.
Om Maharaudraya Namah.
Om Mahabahave Namah.
Om Mahaprabhaya Namah.
Om Kalajnaya Namah.
Om Kalanemighnaya Namah.
Om Karalavadanaya Namah.
Om Shuraya Namah.
Om Vidaranaya Namah.
Om Amitaya Namah. || 50 ||

Om Narayanaya Namah.
Om Daityasudanaya Namah.
Om Mrityumrityave Namah.
Om Sadgataye Namah.
Om Anantaya Namah.
Om Vasudevaya Namah.
Om Vishnave Namah.
Om Prahladapalakaya Namah.
Om Shankha-dharine Namah.
Om Gadapanaye Namah. || 60 ||

Om Chakradharine Namah.
Om Padmahastaya Namah.
Om Vanamaline Namah.
Om Pitambaradharaya Namah.
Om Shrivatsa-ankaya Namah.
Om Kaustubha-dharaya Namah.
Om Garuda-dhvajaya Namah.
Om Kshirabdhi-nilayaya Namah.
Om Shesha-shayine Namah.
Om Pundarikakshaya Namah. || 70 ||

Om Lokanathaya Namah.
Om Jagatpataye Namah.
Om Subrahmanyaya Namah.
Om Bhaktavatsalaya Namah.
Om Sarvajnaya Namah.
Om Sarvapalakaya Namah.
Om Brahmarupaya Namah.
Om Shivarupaya Namah.
Om Vishvarupaya Namah.
Om Sarvadharaya Namah. || 80 ||

Om Jagadvyapine Namah.
Om Jagatkartre Namah.
Om Jagaddhartre Namah.
Om Jagatsamhartre Namah.
Om Pareshaya Namah.
Om Paramatmane Namah.
Om Papavimochanaya Namah.
Om Manovegine Namah.
Om Ameyaya Namah.
Om Dushtasamharakaya Namah. || 90 ||

Om Sarvashastra-dharaya Namah.
Om Sarvavidya-visharadaya Namah.
Om Bhaktarti-nashanaya Namah.
Om Kamitartha-pradaya Namah.
Om Sukhadaya Namah.
Om Sarvamangalaya Namah.
Om Hiranyakashipu-chchhetre Namah.
Om Maha-hiranya-nakhaya Namah.
Om Sarvalokadaraya Namah.
Om Avyayaya Namah. || 100 ||

Om Maharupaya Namah.
Om Mahaprabhave Namah.
Om Anantarupaya Namah.
Om Kalarupaya Namah.
Om Dayanidhaye Namah.
Om Lakshmi-Nrisimhaya Namah.
Om Sarvasiddhi-pradaya Namah.
Om Shri-Nrisimha-Parabrahmane Namah. || 108 ||

|| Iti Shri Narasimha Ashtottara Shatanamavali Sampoorna ||
hindiMeaning: नरसिंह अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली में भगवान नरसिंह के 108 पवित्र नामों को 'ॐ... नमः' प्रारूप में प्रस्तुत किया गया है। इसमें नरसिंह, महासिंह, उग्रसिंह, स्तम्भज, ज्वालामुख, वज्रदंष्ट्र, वज्रनख, मृत्युमृत्यु, प्रह्लादपालक, हिरण्यकशिपुच्छेत्ता, लक्ष्मीनृसिंह आदि नाम हैं। प्रत्येक नाम उनके किसी दिव्य गुण या लीला का स्मरण कराता है। पूजा, अर्चना या नामजप में इसका प्रयोग किया जाता है।
benefits: Ideal for archana (worship) with flowers or kumkum offering 108 names
Invokes powerful protection of lord narasimha
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fears, diseases, and enemies
Invokes the blessing of courage, strength, and divine grace
Especially powerful on Narasimha Jayanti and Tuesdays
description: Narasimha Ashtottara Shatanamavali (नरसिंह अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली) is a devotional recitation of 108 sacred names dedicated to Lord Narasimha. This sacred composition is sourced from Garuda Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to bestow courage, strength, and divine grace as well as remove all fears, diseases, and enemies. Additionally, it is known to grant powerful protection of Lord Narasimha. For best results, devotees chant this on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Narasimha Jayanti. The text contains 108 verses and takes around 12 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Narasimha Ashtottara Shatanamavali in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Narasimha Ashtottara Shatanamavali - 108 names of Lord Narasimha for archana worship with Devanagari, transliteration, and meaning.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Narasimha Ashtottara Shatanamavali and how is it used?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narasimha Ashtottara Shatanamavali (नरसिंह अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली) is the sacred litany of 108 names of Lord Narasimha — the man-lion fourth avatara of Lord Vishnu — preserved in the Garuda Purana and the broader Vaishnava archana tradition. Each name is presented in the namavali format Om [name in dative case] Namah (e.g., Om Narasimhaya Namah, Om Mahasimhaya Namah), suitable for archana — the formal worship in which one flower, tulasi leaf, kumkum dab or akshata grain is offered with each name. The 108 names systematically venerate Narasimha's appearance from the pillar (Stambhaja), his terrifying form (Ugralochana, Karalavadana), his slaying of Hiranyakashipu (Hiranyakashipu-chchetri), and his blessing of Prahlada (Prahlada-palaka). (Source: Garuda Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Narasimha and what is his sacred story?
seoFaqs.answer: Lord Narasimha is the fierce man-lion fourth avatara of Lord Vishnu, who manifested specifically to slay the demon king Hiranyakashipu and protect the supreme bhakta Prahlada (his own son). Hiranyakashipu had obtained from Brahma a boon making him impossible to kill — by man or beast, by day or night, indoors or outdoors, on earth or in the sky, by any weapon. To honour the boon while slaying the tyrant, Vishnu manifested at twilight (neither day nor night) from a pillar (Stambhaja — neither indoors nor outdoors) in a form that was neither man nor beast (man-lion), placed Hiranyakashipu on his lap (neither earth nor sky), and tore him open with his claws (no weapon). It is the supreme story of how Bhagavan protects his devotees by transcending every limit. (Source: Bhagavata Purana 7.8; Vishnu Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is the 108-name format used and how is archana performed?
seoFaqs.answer: The number 108 is supremely sacred in Hindu tradition — corresponding to the 27 nakshatras × 4 padas = 108 stellar quarters, the 12 zodiac signs × 9 planets = 108 grahic combinations, the 108 main marma points in the body, the 108 Upanishads of the Muktika canon, and the 108 beads of the standard japa-mala. The archana ritual involves seating the deity (or simply visualising), then reciting each of the 108 names slowly while offering one flower, one tulasi leaf, one kumkum dab, one akshata grain (or one drop of milk during abhishekam) per name. By the end, 108 offerings have been made, and the devotee has invoked every aspect of the deity by name. It is the heart of Vaishnava puja. (Source: classical Vaishnava archana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What are some of the most powerful names in the Narasimha Ashtottara?
seoFaqs.answer: Several names in the Narasimha Ashtottara carry exceptional power and are often singled out for individual japa: (1) Om Mrityu-mrityave Namah — salutations to the death of death itself (powerful for relief from fear of death and chronic illness); (2) Om Stambha-jaya Namah — to the one who emerged from the pillar (powerful for sudden divine intervention in crisis); (3) Om Vajra-damshtraya Namah and Om Vajra-nakhaya Namah — to the one with thunderbolt-fangs and thunderbolt-claws (supreme protection); (4) Om Hiranyakashipu-chchetre Namah — to the slayer of Hiranyakashipu (destroyer of arrogant enemies); (5) Om Prahlada-palakaya Namah — to the protector of Prahlada (refuge for the devoted); (6) Om Lakshmi-Nrisimhaya Namah — to Lakshmi-Narasimha (the gentle peaceful form). (Source: Narasimha Ashtottara Shatanamavali.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Narasimha Ashtottara Shatanamavali be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The most powerful occasions for Narasimha 108-name archana are: (1) Narasimha Jayanti (Vaishakha Shukla Chaturdashi — the appearance day of Lord Narasimha, the supreme festival), (2) every Saturday (Shanivar — sacred to fierce protective deities and to Narasimha for protection from grahas and shatru), (3) every Swati nakshatra (Narasimha's birth nakshatra), (4) every Chaturdashi tithi (the fourteenth lunar day, sacred to Narasimha), (5) sandhya-kala (twilight — Narasimha's birth time), (6) before any major undertaking facing strong opposition, (7) during any acute fear, illness, or danger, (8) on Vaikuntha Ekadashi and Vishnu festivals. Performed before a Narasimha murti or Saligrama at home or temple, ideally with offerings of red flowers, kumkum and milk-payasa. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana; Narasimha temple paddhati at Ahobilam.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Narasimha Ashtottara Shatanamavali grant?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes extraordinarily powerful benefits to regular recitation of Narasimha's 108 names: (1) absolute protection from all forms of fear — physical, supernatural, karmic, and especially fear of death (Mrityu-mrityu); (2) destruction of all enemies, including malefic grahas, evil spirits, black magic and inner enemies (kama, krodha, lobha); (3) cure of chronic illnesses, especially those that resist medical treatment; (4) protection of children and the family (Narasimha as protector of Prahlada is the supreme child-protector); (5) bestowal of courage, confidence and victory in difficult struggles; (6) for sincere bhaktas, the gentle Lakshmi-Narasimha grace and final liberation; (7) breaking of the most stubborn karmic blocks. He is invoked as the supreme rakshaka. (Sources: Garuda Purana; classical Narasimha upasana tradition.)
